```python
import pytest
# Assuming the function is defined in a module named `lru_cache_module`
from lru_cache_module import lru_cache


class TestLRUCache:
    # Happy Path Tests
    def test_basic_put_and_get(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == 10
        assert get(2) == -1

    def test_update_existing_key(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        put(1, 10)
        put(1, 20)
        assert get(1) == 20

    def test_eviction_when_capacity_exceeded(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        put(3, 3)  # should evict key 1
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 2
        assert get(3) == 3

    def test_lru_order_after_get(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        get(1)       # makes 2 the least recently used
        put(3, 3)    # should evict 2
        assert get(1) == 1
        assert get(2) == -1
        assert get(3) == 3

    def test_lru_order_after_update(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        put(1, 10)   # updates 1, making 2 the least recently used
        put(3, 3)    # should evict 2
        assert get(1) == 10
        assert get(2) == -1
        assert get(3) == 3

    def test_get_returns_negative_one_for_missing_key(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        assert get(99) == -1

    def test_put_with_various_key_types(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(3)
        put("a", 1)
        put(2, "b")
        put(3.14, [1, 2])
        assert get("a") == 1
        assert get(2) == "b"
        assert get(3.14) == [1, 2]

    def test_put_none_key(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        put(None, "value")
        assert get(None) == "value"

    def test_large_capacity(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(1000)
        for i in range(1000):
            put(i, i)
        for i in range(1000):
            assert get(i) == i
        # Exceed capacity
        put(1000, 1000)
        assert get(0) == -1
        assert get(1000) == 1000

    # Edge Case Tests
    def test_capacity_zero(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(0)
        put(1, 1)
        assert get(1) == -1  # cache size never exceeds 0, item evicted immediately

    def test_capacity_one(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(1)
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 2

    def test_negative_capacity_evicts_immediately(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(-1)
        put(1, 1)
        assert get(1) == -1  # len(cache) > -1 always True, pops immediately

    def test_overwrite_only_item_capacity_one(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(1)
        put(1, 1)
        put(1, 2)  # overwrite, should not evict because len never exceeds capacity
        assert get(1) == 2

    # Error Case Tests
    def test_unhashable_key_in_put_raises_type_error(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            put([1, 2], 10)

    def test_unhashable_key_in_get_raises_type_error(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            get([1, 2])

    def test_put_missing_value_raises_type_error(self):
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            put(1)  # missing required argument

    def test_get_missing_argument_raises_type_error(self):
        _, put = lru_cache(2)  # get is callable
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            get()  # no arguments

    def test_lru_cache_with_no_arguments_raises_type_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            lru_cache()
```
